debug_named(3) Library Functions Manual debug_named(3)

NAME

debug_named - Debug_named

SYNOPSIS

Files


file named.c
Named object registry for debugging. file named.h
Named object registry for debugging — log identifiable resource names.

Type-Specific Description Macros

These macros encapsulate the proper casting and type hint for common types. Use these instead of NAMED_DESCRIBE for better readability and consistency.
#define NAME_DESCRIBE_MUTEX(m)
Describe a mutex_t for logging. #define NAME_DESCRIBE_RWLOCK(l)
Describe an rwlock_t for logging. #define NAME_DESCRIBE_COND(c)
Describe a cond_t for logging. #define NAME_DESCRIBE_THREAD(t)
Describe a thread for logging. #define NAME_DESCRIBE_TRANSPORT(tr)
Describe a transport for logging. #define NAME_DESCRIBE_SOCKET(fd)
Describe a socket for logging. #define NAME_DESCRIBE_RINGBUF(rb)
Describe a ring buffer for logging.

Detailed Description

This module provides a centralized registry for naming any addressable resource: mutexes, threads, sockets, ring buffers, network connections, etc.

Any value that fits in a uintptr_t can be registered with a human-readable name. Names are auto-suffixed with a unique ID (e.g., "recv_mutex.7") to distinguish multiple instances of the same conceptual resource.

The registry is thread-safe and zero-overhead in release builds (NDEBUG).

Note

Registry is not thread-safe for unregistration during unwind. Always unregister resources before they become invalid (e.g., in destroy/cleanup).

USAGE EXAMPLES:

// Pointer-based (for heap objects, structs) ringbuffer_t *rb = ringbuffer_create(...); NAMED_REGISTER(rb, "recv_audio"); // auto name: "recv_audio.3" // ... use rb ... NAMED_UNREGISTER(rb); ringbuffer_destroy(rb);

// Integer handle-based (for fd, socket, etc.) int client_fd = accept(server_fd, ...); NAMED_REGISTER_ID(client_fd, "client_socket"); // ... use client_fd ... NAMED_UNREGISTER_ID(client_fd); close(client_fd);

// Describing in logs log_info("Data received on %s", NAME_DESCRIBE_SOCKET(fd)); // Output: "socket: client_socket.2 (0x8)"

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

#define
  NAMED_GET_BY_INT( id, buffer, size)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  const char *_name = named_get((uintptr_t)(id));                                                                   \


  if (_name) {                                                                                                       \


    strncpy((buffer), (_name), (size) - 1);                                                                         \


    (buffer)[(size) - 1] = '\0';                                                                                    \


  } else {                                                                                                           \


    snprintf((buffer), (size), "0x%lx", (unsigned long)(id));                                                       \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Get name of an integer ID or format as hex fallback.


Parameters


id The integer ID to look up (e.g., thread ID, FD,
  packet type)


buffer Output buffer for the result


size Size of the output buffer


Tries to look up the registered name for an integer ID. If found,
    copies it to buffer. If not found, formats the ID as "0x%lx"
    (hexadecimal).




Use this for thread IDs and other small integer identifiers. In
    release builds (NDEBUG), always formats as hex.

#define
  NAMED_GET_BY_PTR( key, buffer, size)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  const char *_name = named_get(key);                                                                               \


  if (_name) {                                                                                                       \


    strncpy((buffer), (_name), (size) - 1);                                                                         \


    (buffer)[(size) - 1] = '\0';                                                                                    \


  } else {                                                                                                           \


    snprintf((buffer), (size), "0x%tx", (ptrdiff_t)(key));                                                          \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Get name of a pointer/key or format as address fallback.


Parameters


key The uintptr_t key to look up


buffer Output buffer for the result


size Size of the output buffer


Tries to look up the registered name for the key. If found, copies
    it to buffer. If not found, formats the key as "0x%tx"
    (hexadecimal address).




Useful in hot paths like logging where you want the name if
    available, but need a reasonable fallback for unregistered keys.




In release builds (NDEBUG), always formats as address.

Copy a string to buffer, with bounds checking.


Parameters


str The string to copy (may be NULL)


buffer Output buffer


size Size of the output buffer


Safe string copy that handles NULL inputs and ensures null
    termination. Useful for formatting optional or variable string fields.




In release builds (NDEBUG), copies the string as-is.

#define
  NAMED_OPEN( pathname, name, flags, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>Value:


({                                                                                                                   \


  int _fd;                                                                                                           \


  if ((flags) & O_CREAT) {                                                                                           \


    _fd = platform_open((pathname), (flags), __VA_ARGS__);                                                           \


  } else {                                                                                                           \


    _fd = platform_open((pathname), (flags));                                                                        \


  }                                                                                                                  \


  if (_fd >= 0) {                                                                                                    \


    named_register_fd(_fd, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__);                                                            \


  }                                                                                                                  \


  _fd;                                                                                                               \
})






Open a file and register the file descriptor.


Parameters


pathname File path to open


name Debug name for the file descriptor


flags Open flags (O_RDONLY, O_WRONLY, etc.)


... Optional mode argument for O_CREAT


Convenience macro that opens a file and registers the returned FD.
    Automatically uses "%d" format specifier for file descriptor
    integers.




Usage: int fd = NAMED_OPEN("/path/to/file",
    "myfile", O_RDONLY); int fd =
    NAMED_OPEN("/path/to/file", "myfile", O_CREAT |
    O_WRONLY, 0644);

703   })










#define
  NAMED_REGISTER( ptr, name, type, fmt, parent_ptr)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>Value:


named_register((uintptr_t)(const void *)(ptr), (name), (type), (fmt), __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, (uintptr_t)(const void *)(parent_ptr))






Register any pointer with base name, type, format spec, and
    location (auto-suffix).


Parameters


ptr Object pointer (void* or typed pointer)


name Base name string


type Data type label (e.g., "mutex", "socket")


fmt Format specifier for printing (e.g., "0x%tx")


In debug builds, automatically captures FILE, LINE,
    and func for location info. In release builds (NDEBUG), this is a
    no-op.

const
  char * named_describe (uintptr_t key, const char * type_hint)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>


Format a description string for logging.


Parameters


key The resource key


type_hint A type label (e.g., "mutex", "socket",
  "thread")


Returns


Description string in format "type_hint: name
  (0xKEY)" or type_hint if not registered


Returns a per-thread static buffer suitable for a single log call.
    The buffer is overwritten on the next call to this function within the same
    thread. Do not store or free the returned pointer.




If the key is not registered, returns just the type_hint. In
    release builds (NDEBUG), this always returns "?".


Definition at line 352 of file debug/named.c.


352                                                                  {
353   if (!type_hint) type_hint = "object";
354 
355   static _Thread_local char buffer[DESCRIBE_BUFFER_SIZE];
356 
357   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
358     snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s (0x%tx)", type_hint, (ptrdiff_t)key);
359     return buffer;
360   }
361 
362   rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
363   named_entry_t *entry = NULL;
364   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
365   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
366 
367   if (entry) {
368     const char *type = entry->type ? entry->type : type_hint;
369     const char *name = entry->name ? entry->name : "unknown";
370     if (entry->file && entry->func && entry->line > 0) {
371       snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s/%s (0x%tx) @ %s:%d:%s()", type, name, (ptrdiff_t)key,
372                entry->file, entry->line, entry->func);
373     } else {
374       snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s/%s (0x%tx)", type, name, (ptrdiff_t)key);
375     }
376   } else {
377     snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s (0x%tx)", type_hint ? type_hint : "unknown", (ptrdiff_t)key);
378   }
379 
380   return buffer;
381 }

void
  named_destroy (void )


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>


Destroy the named object registry. Cleans up all registered names
    and internal structures. Typically called from lock_debug_destroy().


Definition at line 100 of file debug/named.c.


100                          {
101   // Always attempt to shut down the lifecycle, but always cleanup entries
102   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_named_registry.lifecycle);
103 
104   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
105 
106   // Find the registry's own rwlock entry (allocated during named_init bootstrap)
107   // This entry will be unregistered by rwlock_destroy, so we must not free it here
108   uintptr_t registry_lock_key = (uintptr_t)(const void *)&g_named_registry.entries_lock;
109   named_entry_t *registry_lock_entry = NULL;
110   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &registry_lock_key, sizeof(uintptr_t), registry_lock_entry);
111 
112   for (named_entry_t *e = g_named_registry.entries; e != NULL;) {
113     named_entry_t *next = e->hh.next;
114 
115     // Skip the registry lock entry - it will be cleaned up by rwlock_destroy
116     // which calls NAMED_UNREGISTER after we release the lock
117     if (e == registry_lock_entry) {
118       e = next;
119       continue;
120     }
121 
122     // Free all other entries
123     free(e->name);
124     if (e->type) free(e->type);
125     if (e->format_spec) free(e->format_spec);
126     if (e->file) free(e->file);
127     if (e->func) free(e->func);
128     free(e);
129     e = next;
130   }
131 
132   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
133 
134   // rwlock_destroy will call NAMED_UNREGISTER on the lock entry, which will:
135   // 1. Acquire the lock (now safe since we released it)
136   // 2. Find and remove the lock entry from the hash
137   // 3. Free the lock entry's fields
138   rwlock_destroy(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
139 }






References lifecycle_shutdown(), rwlock_destroy(),
    rwlock_wrlock, and rwlock_wrunlock.

#include

    calls. Typically called from lock_debug_init().


Definition at line 81 of file debug/named.c.


81                                    {
82   // Idempotent: if already initialized, return success
83   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
84     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
85   }
86 
87   if (!lifecycle_init(&g_named_registry.lifecycle, "named_registry")) {
88     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
89   }
90 
91   if (rwlock_init(&g_named_registry.entries_lock, "named_registry_lock") != 0) {
92     lifecycle_shutdown(&g_named_registry.lifecycle);
93     return ASCIICHAT_OK;  // Continue even if rwlock init fails

const
  char * named_register (uintptr_t key, const char * base_name, const char *
  type, const char * format_spec, const char * file, int line, const char *
  func, uintptr_t parent_key)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>


Register a resource with an auto-suffixed name, type, and location
    info.


Parameters


key A uintptr_t representing the resource (pointer
  or integer handle)


base_name The base name (e.g., "recv"); suffix auto-generated


type Data type label (e.g., "mutex", "rwlock",
  "socket"); stored and printed


file Source file where registration occurred (typically FILE)


line Source line where registration occurred (typically LINE)


func Function where registration occurred (typically func)


Returns


Pointer to the full registered name (e.g.,
  "recv.7"), valid until unregister


The returned name string is stored in the registry and remains
    valid until named_unregister(key) is called. Do not modify or free the
    returned pointer.




The type parameter is stored in the registry and used by
    named_describe() to format output as "type: name (0xKEY) @
    file:line:func()" automatically, without needing a type_hint
  parameter.




Multiple registrations of the same key will overwrite the previous
    entry. In release builds (NDEBUG), this is a no-op and returns
  base_name.


Definition at line 175 of file debug/named.c.


176                                                                                                {
177 #ifdef NDEBUG
178   // In release builds, skip debug registry and return the base name
179   return base_name ? base_name : "?";
180 #endif
181 
182   if (!base_name || !type || !format_spec) {
183     return "?";
184   }
185 
186   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
187     return base_name;
188   }
189 
190   // If parent_key provided, look up parent name and create hierarchical name
191   char final_base_name[256];
192   if (parent_key != 0) {
193     rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
194     const char *parent_name = named_lookup_name_unlocked(parent_key);
195     rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
196 
197     if (parent_name) {
198       snprintf(final_base_name, sizeof(final_base_name), "%s#%s", parent_name, base_name);
199     } else {
200       // Parent not found, fall back to base name
201       snprintf(final_base_name, sizeof(final_base_name), "%s", base_name);
202     }
203   } else {
204     snprintf(final_base_name, sizeof(final_base_name), "%s", base_name);
205   }
206 
207   // Generate unique name using atomic counters (only for non-hierarchical names)
208   char name_buffer[256];
209   if (parent_key == 0) {
210     uint64_t counter = 0;
211     if (strcmp(type, "mutex") == 0) {
212       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&mutex_counter, 1);
213     } else if (strcmp(type, "rwlock") == 0) {
214       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&rwlock_counter, 1);
215     } else if (strcmp(type, "cond") == 0) {
216       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&cond_counter, 1);
217     } else if (strcmp(type, "atomic") == 0) {
218       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&atomic_counter, 1);
219     }
220     snprintf(name_buffer, sizeof(name_buffer), "%s.%"PRIu64, final_base_name, counter);
221   } else {
222     // Hierarchical names don't get counters - parent already has one
223     snprintf(name_buffer, sizeof(name_buffer), "%s", final_base_name);
224   }
225 
226   // Allocate entry BEFORE acquiring lock (avoid long critical section)
227   named_entry_t *entry = malloc(sizeof(named_entry_t));
228   if (!entry) return base_name;
229 
230   entry->key = key;
231   entry->name = entry_strdup(name_buffer);
232   named_entry_init(entry, type, format_spec, file, line, func);
233 
234   // Only hold lock for the hash table operation
235   log_dev("[NAMED_REGISTER_LOCK_1] About to acquire entries_lock for key=%p type=%s", (void*)key, type);
236   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
237   log_dev("[NAMED_REGISTER_LOCK_2] ✅ Acquired entries_lock, adding hash entry");
238   HASH_ADD(hh, g_named_registry.entries, key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
239   log_dev("[NAMED_REGISTER_LOCK_3] Hash entry added, releasing entries_lock");
240   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
241   log_dev("[NAMED_REGISTER_LOCK_4] ✅ Released entries_lock");
242 
243   return entry->name;
244 }

    lifecycle_is_initialized(), log_dev, named_entry::name,

    rwlock_wrunlock.

Registers an FD with a key that includes type namespace to avoid
    collisions with packet types or other numeric values. Name is auto-generated
    as "fd=%d". In release builds (NDEBUG), this is a no-op and
    returns "fd=%d" string.

#include

Register a resource with a formatted name, type, and location info
    (no auto-suffix).


Parameters


key A uintptr_t representing the resource


type Data type label (e.g., "mutex", "socket");
  stored and printed


file Source file where registration occurred


line Source line where registration occurred


func Function where registration occurred


fmt Printf-style format string


... Format arguments


Returns


Pointer to the full registered name


Allows full control over the registered name. The format string
    and arguments are printed to create the name (e.g., "client.%u"
    with args "17" → "client.17"). No auto-suffix
    counter is applied.




The type parameter is stored in the registry and used by

  type_hint.

254   }
255 
256   // Format the name BEFORE acquiring lock

261   va_end(args);
262

264     return "?";

266 
267   // Allocate entry BEFORE acquiring lock
268   named_entry_t *entry = malloc(sizeof(named_entry_t));
269   if (!entry) {
270     free(full_name);
271     return "?";
272   }
273 
274   // Initialize entry (name is pre-allocated as full_name)
275   entry->key = key;
276   entry->name = full_name;
277   named_entry_init(entry, type, format_spec, file, line, func);
278 
279   // Only hold lock for the hash table operation

282   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
283 
284   return entry->name;
285 }

void
  named_registry_for_each (named_iter_callback_t callback, void *
  user_data)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>


Iterate through all registered entries.


Parameters


callback Function to call for each entry


user_data Opaque context passed to callback


Safely iterates through all registered entries without holding the
    lock for the entire iteration (entries are copied). Callback is invoked for
    each entry. In release builds (NDEBUG), this is a no-op.


Definition at line 393 of file debug/named.c.


393                                                                               {
394   if (!callback || !lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
395     return;
396   }
397 
398   // Snapshot entries while holding read lock
399   named_iter_entry_t entries[256];
400   int count = 0;
401 
402   rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
403   for (named_entry_t *e = g_named_registry.entries; e != NULL && count < 256; e = e->hh.next) {
404     entries[count].key = e->key;
405     strncpy(entries[count].name, e->name ? e->name : "?", MAX_NAME_LEN - 1);
406     entries[count].name[MAX_NAME_LEN - 1] = '\0';
407     count++;
408   }
409   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
410 
411   // Call callback outside the lock
412   for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
413     callback(entries[i].key, entries[i].name, user_data);
414   }
415 }

Register all packet types from packet_type_t enum.

key The same uintptr_t passed to

293   named_entry_t *entry = NULL;
294   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);

const
  char * named_update_name (uintptr_t key, const char * new_base_name)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/debug/named.h>


Update the registered name for a resource with a new base
  name.


Parameters


key The resource key


new_base_name New base name (will be auto-suffixed with counter)


Returns


Updated name string, or NULL if key not found


Updates an existing registration with a new base name. The new
    name will be auto-suffixed with a counter to make it unique (e.g.,
    "client_123.0"). Useful when a resource gets an ID assigned after
    creation (e.g., client_id).
